author: xooooooooox
description: Selective, on-demand distribution of vault configuration (snippets, hotkeys, plugin settings) across devices and vaults. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: xooooooooox
manifest.description: Selective, on-demand distribution of vault configuration (snippets, hotkeys, plugin settings) across devices and vaults.
manifest.name: Config Sync

name: Config Sync

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## The ribbon menu opens instantly, and encrypted-field checks stop burning CPU

- **Instant menu:** clicking the Config Sync ribbon icon previously ran a full status scan before the menu could appear — on vaults with many items (especially encrypted-field ones) that meant a visible wait. The menu now opens immediately with counts from the most recent check, and the scan refreshes in the background.
- **Key derivation cache:** every stored encrypted-field envelope reuses a fixed salt, yet each status refresh re-derived the same PBKDF2 key (210k iterations) over and over. Derived keys are now memoized, so after the first scan, refreshes — panel opens, the periodic check, awareness events — skip the expensive derivation entirely.

Node suite at 326 tests.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Counts match what the filters show

Fixes a 0.23.4 follow-up: a version-ahead item correctly appeared under **To capture**, but every count still said 0 — the filter pill, the header badges, and the sidebar badges were counting raw states instead of presented ones. All user-facing counts now agree with what the filters actually display.

Node suite at 326 tests.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## See what changed, see your passphrase state, and version bumps propagate again

- **Inline diffs:** every change line in a Sync Center row (`~ data.json`) is now clickable — it expands a git-style diff right below, showing exactly what the pending capture or apply would write vs what's there now. Unified or side-by-side view; field-protected items diff in their protected form; encrypted items stay undisclosed.
- **Version updates propagate:** updating a plugin used to leave the store's recorded version stale forever if its settings hadn't changed — other devices' "Outdated on this device" flow never fired. An item whose local version is newer than the store's now surfaces under **To capture** with an amber hint; capturing refreshes the store version (no content is rewritten) so your other devices see the update.
- **Passphrase at a glance:** the General tab now shows a clear badge — green "✓ Set on this device" or amber "Not set" — instead of a faint status buried in the description.

Node suite at 326 tests.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## "Update & apply" now really does both in one pass

Fixes the Outdated/Disabled/Not-installed sections' combined actions: choosing **Apply store** together with **Update to latest** (or Enable / Install & enable) previously updated the plugin but the applied settings could be silently overwritten — you had to stage and apply a second time.

- Root cause: the plugin was re-enabled **before** its settings file was written. A loading plugin reads its data.json — and many plugins re-save it shortly after startup — so the just-applied settings got clobbered by the plugin's stale in-memory state.
- Now every enable (from Update, Install & enable, and Enable actions) runs **after** the config write: the plugin starts up already holding the applied settings. One staging pass, one Apply.

Node suite at 323 tests.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Excluded plugins are truly hands-off, and the exclusion list is sorted

- **Pass-through capture for exclusions:** capturing a plugin on/off list now leaves an excluded plugin's entry in the shared list exactly as it was — your device neither adds nor removes it. Previously, a capture triggered by an unrelated list change could silently delete an excluded plugin's entry from the shared list (surprising you, and fighting any device that legitimately syncs that plugin). Removing an id from the shared list is now always an explicit, un-excluded operation.
- The "Excluded from this list" rows are now sorted by plugin display name instead of internal id — easier to find things.

Node suite at 321 tests.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Smoother panel interactions, and sensitive items default to protected fields

- **No more flashing:** toggling a plugin's **excluded** state on the on/off list items, and switching an item's device scope (all devices → desktop only), now update in place instead of re-rendering the panel — the visible flicker is gone. A failed save still restores the real value and shows the inline error.
- **Sensitive items enable protected:** turning on sync for an item flagged with **⚠ detected keys** now defaults to **Fields** mode with rules seeded from the detection — key names matching apiKey/token/secret/password/credential become **encrypt** rules, other detected keys become **strip** rules — instead of starting as plain. Existing synced items are unchanged; you can still switch any item's mode manually.

Node suite at 321 tests.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Per-device exclusions for the plugin on/off lists

The two switch-list items — **Enabled community plugins** and the **core plugin on/off list** — previously mirrored the whole list, so devices with legitimately different plugin sets could never stabilize: every capture/apply flipped someone's choices back. Now each device can carve specific plugins out of the shared list.

### Exclude a plugin from the shared list
- Expand the on/off list item in the config panel → **"Excluded from this list (this device)"**: every known plugin (local list ∪ store ∪ installed) with an **included ↔ excluded** toggle. Excluded plugins keep their own on/off state on this device — the shared list neither includes nor changes them.
- Exclusions work symmetrically, like field strip rules: an excluded plugin **never enters the store at capture** (your local decisions don't impose on other devices), **keeps its local state on apply**, and is **masked out of the in-sync comparison** — so the item finally shows a stable ✓ across heterogeneous devices.
- The exclusion list is per-device (a new locked `switchExceptions` preset on the Config Sync item keeps it out of self-sync), and it's independent of the plugin's own settings item — "settings shared everywhere, enabled only here" is a supported combination.

### Presentation
- The item shows an **"N excluded"** badge in the config panel and a `· N excluded` hint in the Sync Center; the row expansion lists each exclusion with a `⌂` marker.
- The on/off list items' expansion is now focused: just the exclusion editor (the raw-JSON viewer and Custom location made no sense for these fixed-path lists and are gone). Their mode is pinned to Plain.

With no exclusions configured, behavior is byte-for-byte identical to before.

Node suite at 321 tests; minAppVersion 1.8.7.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Staged counts no longer include already-synced items

Fixes the Sync Center footer showing inflated counts (e.g. "Apply 36 items" right after applying those 36 items).

- Staging now expires with the state that motivated it: when an item becomes inert (in sync, no settings, or locked) it drops out of the staged set automatically — previously items you had just captured or applied stayed silently staged and kept counting toward the **Apply** button.
- The footer's staged counts and the Capture/Apply payloads now count only rows that can actually be staged, matching what the checkboxes allow.

Node suite at 243 tests.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## One config file, merge-based pull with conflict resolution, and fresh-device adoption

A structural release: config-sync's own configuration is now a single file, pull no longer overwrites your local setup, and a new device can adopt an existing configuration in one click.

### Your sync contract lives in the plugin settings
- The list of synced items (formerly `config-sync.json` in the store folder) now lives inside the plugin's own `data.json` — one configuration, one place. On first load the old file is imported automatically and renamed to `config-sync.json.migrated-<timestamp>` as an auditable backup.
- The store folder now holds only the captured copies (`store/**`) and capture metadata (`store.lock.json`).

### The plugin syncs itself, safely
- **Config Sync** stays a normal synced item: capturing it carries your item list and portable preferences to other devices through the store.
- Its per-device fields — the store folder path and your remotes — are protected by **locked preset rules** (🔒 in the Fields list): they are removed from the captured copy entirely and each device keeps its own values on apply. Field rules gain a generic `locked` flag.
- Fields marked **Strip** now read as what they are: "don't sync this field" — removed at capture, local value kept on apply.

### Pull is a merge, not a mirror
- Pulling from a remote now **merges**: items and files that exist only on this device are **never deleted** (previously a pull could wipe a freshly configured device), one-sided changes merge automatically, and only genuine both-sides-changed differences pause for you.
- Conflicts open a **git-style resolution dialog**: every conflict shows a real diff (unified or side-by-side view), you pick **Local** or **Remote** per row — with "all local / all remote" shortcuts — and nothing is written until you apply. Cancel writes nothing at all.
- Push no longer writes a root manifest file; remotes with the old layout are still readable.

### New device? Adopt the configuration
- When a fresh install finds an existing configuration in the store (e.g. brought over by your regular vault sync), the Sync Center offers a one-click **Adopt** — your item list and preferences arrive; the device-local store path and remotes stay untouched.

### Also fixed
- Remote checks and the store-folder browser now recognize the new store layout (previously a store without the old root manifest reported "no store").

Node suite at 242 tests; minAppVersion 1.8.7.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Sync Center checkbox column: aligned select-all, clearer disabled state

Two fixes to the Sync Center's checkbox column.

- The top **select all** now right-aligns to the same column as the per-row checkboxes. Previously it sat mid-width, just after the filter pills, because a base `margin: 0` rule was silently overriding its auto margin.
- **Disabled** checkboxes now read as clearly disabled — the top select-all when nothing is selectable, and in-sync rows — with a dimmed box and a `not-allowed` cursor, instead of looking identical to a normal empty box.

Pure CSS; no behavior change, no color introduced. Node suite at 207 tests.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Git connectivity check, live remote name, and unified "Store" wording

A pass over the Remotes tab and the storage-location terminology.

### Test a git remote before you rely on it
- Git remotes get a **Test connection** button (desktop) that runs a lightweight `git ls-remote` — reachability and auth, no download — and shows the result inline: reachable with the branch found, reachable but the branch is missing, or the git error surfaced (e.g. `Permission denied (publickey)`). The result clears when you edit the URL or branch.

### The remote name updates as you type
- Typing a remote's name now updates its row header live, instead of showing `(unnamed)` until the next refresh.

### One word for "where the store lives"
- Unified under **Store**: General's "Data folder" → **Store folder**, the git remote's "Folder in repo" → **Store folder in repo**; the vault remote's "Store path" was already consistent.

No remote data-model change. Node suite at 207 tests.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Core plugins are enumerated from the running Obsidian

Core-plugin recognition was driven by a fixed, hand-maintained table of ids. Any core plugin missing from that table — notably **Quick switcher** (`switcher`) — never appeared in the Sync Center and wouldn't classify correctly.

- Core plugins are now read from Obsidian's live internal-plugin registry, injected at load, so **any** core plugin the running app ships (now or in a future Obsidian version) is listed, classified, labeled, and path-validated — no code change needed when Obsidian adds one.
- The Core section lists only core plugins whose settings file actually exists in your config folder, keeping the ~dozen UI-only core plugins (file explorer, global search, outline, …) out of the list.
- Quick switcher now shows up as expected.

No settings or data-model change. Node suite at 204 tests.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Display names fill in on any refresh, not just on Capture

Synced items carry an optional display-name `label` in `config-sync.json` so they read correctly (e.g. `Dataview`, `App settings`) even on a device where the plugin isn't installed. Previously that label was only backfilled when you ran **Capture**, so after an upgrade — or on a device that hadn't captured yet — labels stayed empty.

- The label backfill now runs from the normal status refresh (panel open, the periodic check, and store-awareness events), so labels populate on the next refresh without needing a Capture.
- The enable-time write is unchanged: an item still shows its name the moment you turn its sync on.
- Purely a timing fix — no data-model or settings change. Node suite at 202 tests.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Settings reliability, sensitive workflow, and a theme-native design system

A big config-panel pass: saving is now atomic, sensitive keys surface before you enable sync, the panel is fully theme-native, and each synced item gets a proper expansion.

### Saving is transactional
- Every settings change writes atomically and **rolls back in memory** on a failed disk write, showing the error inline on the offending card instead of only at the page bottom.
- The group-name validator accepts uppercase plugin ids (e.g. `plugin-DEVONlink-obsidian`), so one bad name no longer poisons every save.

### Sensitive keys, handled before you enable
- Detection runs on **every installed plugin** before you turn its sync on, floating flagged items to the top of their section with a `⚠ N keys` badge.
- Each synced row expands into: **Fields to protect**, a read-only **View data.json** (keys colored by rule state — click one to add a strip/encrypt rule, the escape hatch when detection misses a field), and **Custom location**.

### Custom location, clearer badges
- The old "Advanced" sub-section is now **Custom location** — a default-collapsed row of **Location · Type · Path** for overriding where an item is stored, plus **Reset to default**.
- The `⚙ custom location` badge appears **only** when an item uses a non-default storage location. Choosing an encryption mode, a device scope, or field rules is everyday configuration and no longer flags the item.

### Cleaner classification
- `workspaces.json` is reclassified as the **Workspaces** core plugin; volatile `workspace.json` / `workspace-mobile.json` move to Advanced → Discovered files.
- The "Not recommended" section dissolves into inline **`device-specific`** badges (Sync, Publish) that still prompt a caution on enable.
- The Advanced tab is slimmed to Custom rules, Discovered files, and a customized-rules summary banner.

### Theme-native design system
- The panel reuses Obsidian's native components without overriding their surfaces, carries **zero hardcoded colors** (enforced by a new `check-no-hardcoded-color.sh` gate), and binds every semantic accent (encrypt/strip/detected, hub strips/sections) to Obsidian's palette variables — so the panel and Sync Center render correctly in **any theme** automatically. Verified in the default theme and AnuPpuccin.

### Display names on disk
- `config-sync.json` groups gain an optional `label`, written on enable and backfilled on capture, so items read correctly (e.g. `Dataview`) even on a device where the plugin isn't installed.

Node suite at 202 tests; minAppVersion 1.8.7.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Availability sections, plugin install engine, and inline reports

Apply is now availability-aware: the Sync Center understands whether each plugin is enabled, disabled, outdated, or missing on this device — and can fix that as part of applying.

### Sync Center: four availability layers

- The main list now holds only items that are ready to use. Three opt-in, collapsed sections sit below it:
  - **Outdated on this device** — enabled plugins whose store settings were captured on a newer plugin version.
  - **Disabled on this device** — installed but turned off.
  - **Not installed on this device** — the store has settings, this device doesn't have the plugin.
- Section items are never staged or counted until you opt in; all top-level numbers reflect the main list only.
- Checkbox = whether settings sync. A new **On apply** segment = what happens to the plugin itself, composed from the item's actual gaps:
  - Outdated: `⤓ Update to latest` (default) | `Keep {version}`
  - Disabled: `⏻ Enable` (default) | `Keep disabled` — and `⤓ Update & enable` when it's also outdated
  - Not installed: `⤓ Install & enable` (default) | `⤓ Install` | `Stage only`

### Plugin install engine

- Applying can install or update community plugins directly from the official community catalog (latest release, works on mobile).
- Failures are isolated and honest: a failed update leaves the existing settings untouched; a failed install still stages the settings so they take effect when you install manually. Plugins that aren't in the catalog (or whose latest release identifies as a different plugin) are staged with a clear note.
- Enables triggered from the panel persist across restarts, and every outcome is verified — no false "enabled" reports.

### No more warning walls

- The pre-apply "version warnings" dialog is gone. Everything it warned about is now visible in the panel before you click Apply, and version drift is handled where it belongs:
  - Plugin behind the store → the Outdated section, with a one-click update path.
  - Plugin ahead of the store → a quiet metadata line ("capturing will refresh the store").
  - Obsidian and core-plugin settings are anchored to the Obsidian app version — reminder text only.
- Apply always runs directly; the only dialog left in the plugin is the Revert report.

### Inline results

- Capture, Apply, Pull, and Push results render as a dismissible strip at the top of the panel instead of a modal — expand `details` for the full report, including per-item outcome pills like `⤓ installed & enabled 0.5.68`, `⏻ enabled`, `staged for install`.
- Report styling (shared with the Revert dialog): a `+ added · ~ updated · − deleted` legend, per-section counts, and tooltips on every change chip.

### Search

- The Sync Center's name filter moved to the sidebar and now searches **all** scopes at once: sidebar entries show hit counts, and sections auto-expand to show matches.

### Under the hood

- `store.lock.json` now records the Obsidian app version for settings/core items (`sourceAppVersion`) alongside plugin versions.
- 187 node tests (up from 166).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Names you recognize, search that finds everything, and errors that speak plainly.

## New
- **Plugin names everywhere.** Every list — settings tabs, the Sync Center, reports, warnings — now shows real names (`BRAT`, `Templater`, `Daily notes`) instead of ids like `plugin-obsidian42-brat`. Not-installed plugins fall back to their id without the prefix; hover a row to see the underlying path. The Sync Center search matches both the visible name and the id.
- **Search all settings.** The settings search now covers everything — General options, all three picker tabs, Advanced rules, discovered files, and your remotes — with scope pills (`All / General / Obsidian / Core / Community / Advanced / Remotes`) to narrow results. Clicking a non-item result jumps to its tab and highlights the setting.

## Changed
- Advanced's "Managed by pickers" is now **Synced items**, grouped into the same Obsidian / Core plugins / Community plugins categories as the rest of the panel, with plain-name card titles.
- **Validation errors speak product language.** All 29 settings-surfaced messages were rewritten: no more `Not saved: Invalid config-sync data:` prefixes — each error now says which item, what's expected, and gives an example (e.g. `The store path for "nas" needs to be a full path starting with / or ~/ — for example ~/Vaults/other-vault/config-sync.`).

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.18.1...0.19.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Mobile typing fix.

## Fixed
- **The settings search box no longer bounces the keyboard.** Typing in `Search all settings…` used to rebuild the whole settings tab on every keystroke — on phones that collapsed and re-opened the on-screen keyboard once per character. The search box now stays put and only the results below it refresh, so the keyboard stays open and focus never moves. (Desktop gets the same smoother behavior, it was just less noticeable there.)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.18.0...0.18.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
No more blind waits, no more shattered phone rows, no more crying wolf on theme authors.

## New
- **Live progress on Capture and Apply.** The footer button now ticks `Capturing 12/57…` with a spinner and a thin progress bar while a batch runs; hover it to see which item is being processed. Both buttons stay locked until the run finishes — including across any automatic panel refresh that lands mid-run.

## Fixed
- **Phone settings rows.** On phones the item rows stack: name and detection badge on top, description full-width, and the device/mode/toggle controls on their own row — nothing clips or wraps one-word-per-line anymore.
- **`author` is not a secret.** The sensitive-key detector no longer flags `author` / `authorUrl` (present in every theme and plugin manifest); real credential keys like `authToken` and `oauth` are still caught.
- **Settings no longer flash.** Choosing `Fields`/`Encrypt` or editing field rules updates just that row in place instead of rebuilding the whole settings tab.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.17.1...0.18.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Mobile hardening patch, following a full audit of the plugin on Obsidian mobile.

## Fixed
- **Comfortable touch targets on phones and tablets.** Staging checkboxes now have a ~39px effective hit area (previously 15px), and filter pills, direction segments, sidebar entries and the fold lines all meet a 36px minimum; item rows reach 44px. Desktop is unchanged.
- **Clear error when WebCrypto is missing.** On the rare webview without `crypto.subtle`, Encrypt-mode operations now fail with `WebCrypto is unavailable in this environment — Encrypt modes cannot run on this device` instead of a raw TypeError.
- Defense-in-depth: the desktop-only Remotes settings tab can never render on mobile even if its state is reached indirectly.

The audit found the rest of the mobile posture already sound: all Node/Electron access is desktop-gated behind dynamic imports, remotes are hidden on mobile, and encryption/passphrase features work in mobile webviews.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.17.0...0.17.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Sensitive settings can finally travel: every item now has a sync mode you choose — including real encryption.

## New
- **Per-item sync modes.** In Settings, each item offers `Plain | Fields | Encrypt`:
  - **Plain** — synced as-is (default, unchanged).
  - **Fields** — per-key rules: **Strip** keeps a key out of the store entirely (Apply preserves your local value); **Encrypt** stores just that value as ciphertext and decrypts it on Apply. Perfect for configs that are 90% preferences and a couple of API keys.
  - **Encrypt** — the whole file is stored encrypted (AES-256-GCM, key derived from your passphrase).
- **Vault passphrase.** One password field in Settings → General, entered once per device — never written to any file, never synced. The same passphrase on each device is all you need; envelopes carry their own salt/IV. Encrypted items without a passphrase show a 🔒 *locked* state and refuse to capture/apply until you set it; a wrong passphrase fails cleanly without touching your files.
- **Detection that informs, not decides.** Settings rows warn when a config contains sensitive-looking keys (`⚠ N sensitive-looking keys`) or is one opaque encrypted blob — and prefill sensible Fields rules when you opt in. What actually syncs is always your call.
- **Blacklist removed.** The "Not recommended — cannot be synced" wall is gone: remotely-save, ioto-update, slides-rup and config-sync itself are now ordinary items. Whole-file-encrypt remotely-save; Fields-mode the IOTO plugins; you decide.
- The Sync Center badges each item's mode (`🔒` whole-file, `▤` fields) and capture reports state exactly what was encrypted or stripped — the store stays auditable.

## Changed
- The `sanitize` config field is replaced by `mode: "fields"` with `fields` rules (strip/encrypt). Old configs containing `sanitize` fail validation with a message telling you the one-line rename.
- Minimum Obsidian version is now **1.8.7** (device-local passphrase storage uses a newer API). 0.16.0 remains the latest release for older Obsidian.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.16.0...0.17.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
The Sync panel becomes the **Sync Center** — a real workspace tab that stays open, stays fresh, and uses your whole window.

## New
- **Sync Center tab.** The ribbon's *Sync…* and the open command now open (or focus — never duplicate) a persistent full-width workspace tab instead of a modal. Everything from 0.15.0 — scope sidebar, filters, search, staging, remote comparisons — is there, with room to breathe.
- **It keeps itself fresh.** The view reloads when it gains focus and whenever the plugin's awareness notices changes (store edits, periodic checks, remote auto-checks). A `refreshed …` stamp in the header shows when. The ribbon dot and the center are finally one loop: when the dot lights up, the tab you switch to is already up to date.
- **Your work-in-progress survives refreshes.** Scope, search, expanded rows, and everything you've staged stay put across automatic reloads (items that disappear are pruned). The automatic pre-check of changed items only happens when the tab first opens — a background refresh never re-ticks things behind your back.
- **Compact mode for narrow panes.** Drag the tab narrow (or open it on a phone) and the sidebar folds into a scope switcher button with a dropdown — same entries, same badges. This now reacts to *any* resize, tracked per-pane rather than per-window.

## Fixed
- Unstaged `↑ Capture / ↓ Apply store` toggle segments regain their orange/purple direction colors (lost in 0.15.0).
- The select-all checkbox lines up with the row checkboxes below it.

## Note
- Minimum Obsidian version is now **1.7.2** (the tab open/focus behavior relies on a newer workspace API). On older Obsidian, 0.15.0 remains the latest installable release.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.15.0...0.16.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
The Sync panel grows up: a wide two-pane layout and one consistent way to act.

## New
- **Two-pane layout (920px).** A scope sidebar on the left — `All items`, each category, and your remotes, each with compact `↑ ↓ ✓ ○` count badges — and the item list on the right. Click a category to focus it; click a remote to see its full comparison with Pull/Push right there. Below 700px the panel folds to a single column with the sidebar as chips.
- **Name search.** Type in `Filter by name…` to narrow the list instantly — useful at 60+ plugins.
- **Direction staging.** Expanded rows now carry a small `↑ Capture | ↓ Apply store` toggle. Clicking it *stages* the item in that direction (the checkbox turns orange or purple) — nothing runs until you press the footer buttons. This also means you can deliberately apply the store version over local edits, item by item, or capture something the panel guessed the other way.
- **One-glance footer.** `N staged` plus the two execute buttons, always visible.

## Changed
- Everything in the panel is now *orchestration*; only the footer `Capture / Apply` buttons execute. The old expand-row buttons that ran immediately are gone.
- In-sync and no-settings rows fold into one line each (`✓ N items in sync`, `○ N items with no settings yet`) — click to unfold.
- Filter pill counts follow the selected sidebar scope; the title pills stay global, so the header always tells the whole story.
- Per-section checkboxes are replaced by a single select-all next to the search box, acting on exactly what you currently see.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.14.0...0.15.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
The panel stops offering actions that can't succeed, and remote comparisons lose their permanent noise line.

## New
- **"No settings yet" state (`○`).** Items with no config file on this device *and* nothing in the store — typically plugins you've never configured — now get their own gray state instead of being lumped into "To capture". They show dimmed with the checkbox disabled, count under a gray `○ N` pill and a `No settings yet` filter, and explain themselves when expanded. The moment such a plugin saves its first settings, the item moves to "To capture" automatically.

## Fixed
- **Capture can no longer error with "nothing to capture yet".** Previously these no-file items were counted and selectable under "To capture" (one vault showed "To capture 16" where only 1 item was real), and capturing them just produced errors. `—` now strictly means "on this device but not in the store yet". The ribbon dot also no longer lights up for them.
- **Remote comparisons drop the `store metadata ~1` noise.** The store's lock file changes on every capture, so that row appeared in nearly every remote diff while saying nothing useful. A remote that differs only in bookkeeping now shows `✓ remote matches the local store`. Pull/Push reports still list metadata writes — those are real.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.13.0...0.14.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
The Sync panel now scales to real-world vaults with 60+ items, and every expanded row finally tells you something.

## New
- **Filter pills.** `All / To capture / To apply / In sync` at the top of the items block — one click to see only what a given action would touch. Filtering changes what's *listed*, never the counts: section summaries, header pills and the action buttons always reflect the full picture.
- **Collapsible sections.** Category heads (Obsidian / Core plugins / Community plugins) fold with one click and carry `↑ ↓ ✓` count summaries, so a folded section still tells you what's inside. Sections that are entirely in sync start folded. Your fold state is remembered while the app is open.
- **In-sync rows collapse to one line.** Instead of a wall of `✓` rows, each section shows `✓ N items in sync ▸` — click to unfold.
- **Per-item actions in the expanded row.** Every changed item now offers `↑ Capture this (keep local)` and `↓ Apply store version (overwrites local)` — so you can push a single item in *either* direction, including applying the store over local edits (previously impossible from the panel).

## Fixed
- **Expanding a row always shows content.** Changed items show their file diff (long lists cap at 10 with `… N more files ▸`), in-sync items say `identical to the store`, never-captured items say `not captured yet — nothing in the store`, and failed comparisons show the error. Previously many rows expanded to nothing.
- The header count pills no longer run under the close button.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.12.2...0.13.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Polish patch for the Sync panel.

## Fixed
- **Newly ticked items now count.** Enabling something new in the pickers lights the ribbon dot orange and shows up in the `↑` counts immediately (previously "not captured yet" items were invisible to the awareness surfaces). The counting model is now uniform everywhere: `↑` = items Capture would save (changed here + never captured), `↓` = items Apply would update (store newer + differs), `✓` = in sync.
- The panel header can no longer claim everything is in sync while `≠ differs` items exist.
- `workspace.json` / `workspaces.json` no longer appear twice (they live only under the Obsidian tab's *Not recommended*).
- Sync panel styling now matches the intended design: clearer layered depth between blocks and cards in any theme, properly drawn orange/purple checkboxes, tidier spacing. Item rows drop the redundant path text (hover a row to see the full path).
- Small grammar fix in remote previews ("1 more item matches").

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.12.1...0.12.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Visual-fidelity patch for the 0.12.0 Sync panel — the layout now matches the design it was built from.

> If you skipped 0.12.0: read [its notes](https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/releases/tag/0.12.0) first — that release consolidated all commands into the Sync panel (breaking; hotkeys need re-binding to **Sync**).

## Fixed
- Header count pills sit on the title line, right-aligned, with their orange/purple/green tints; zero counts are hidden.
- Category select-all checkboxes moved to the section headings' right edge.
- Long item names no longer wrap — rows stay single-line with truncated paths.
- Remote previews: the "N more items match" line and the Pull/Push sentence share one line; a remote that matches the local store now says so instead of offering phantom changes, with both buttons quieted.
- Sync-menu counts render as plain text — `Sync… (↑2 ↓1)` — because desktop ribbon menus are native OS menus that can't display styled badges or icons (a platform limit, now handled deliberately).
- README screenshot refreshed to the corrected panel.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.12.0...0.12.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
> ⚠️ **Breaking — one minute of setup after upgrading:**
> - The command palette now has just **`Sync: open the sync panel`** and **`Revert last apply`**. The old `Capture` / `Apply` / `Pull` / `Push` / `Status` commands are gone — re-bind any hotkeys to **Sync**.
> - Individual ribbon-icon toggles were reset (now: Sync, Revert — off by default).
> - Everything you used those commands for lives in the new **Sync panel**.

## What's new

### The Sync panel — one place for everything
Open it from the ribbon menu (or the `Sync` command). Every synced item is listed by category with a status badge; **checkboxes are color-coded by direction** — orange ticks feed **↑ Capture**, purple ticks feed **↓ Apply** — with items pre-selected when there's something to do. Expand any item to see exactly which files were added / updated / deleted. Remotes are checked automatically and shown with their freshness; expand a remote for a **Pull/Push preview** of what would change, with one-click **Pull** (cyan) / **Push** (pink) buttons.

![Sync panel](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/main/docs/assets/sync-panel.png)

### Always-visible awareness
- A **status dot on the ribbon icon**: orange = you changed settings here (capture them), blue = the store or a remote has updates. The tooltip summarizes what's pending.
- The dot updates by itself: instantly when your note sync delivers store changes, every few minutes for local edits, and remotes are checked shortly after startup and every few hours. Two new toggles under Settings → General control the automatic checks.

### Reports that show the change
Capture/Apply/Pull/Push reports now say what actually changed — per-item `+added ~updated −deleted` chips with expandable file lists — instead of raw copy counts. Unchanged files are no longer rewritten, which also stops needless note-sync churn.

### Smaller fixes
- **Selective capture**: only the items you tick are captured; everything else (including version records) is left untouched.
- The `Editor & general` picker entry is now **App settings**, honestly describing everything `app.json` holds.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.11.0...0.12.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### Know what's in sync
- **Status** (new command + sync-menu item): every group at a glance — `✓ in sync`, `↑ changed on this device`, `↓ store is newer`, `— not captured yet`. Paths shown as real paths on this device. Direction hints are best-effort (based on file times vs the last capture).
- **Remote freshness**: in the Status view, press **Check** next to a remote to see whether it was captured after or before your local store — a quick "should I Pull or Push?" answer.
- **Change counts in the sync menu**: the ribbon menu now reads e.g. `Capture (2 changed here)` / `Apply (1 store-newer)` when there's something to do.
- **Smarter Apply picker**: each group shows its status; groups the store updated are pre-selected, unchanged ones are dimmed, and locally-changed ones warn that applying overwrites your edits. The button counts what you selected.
- Two new toggles under Settings → General let you turn off the menu counts or picker status if you prefer speed over hints (both on by default).

### Fixed
- macOS `.DS_Store` (and Windows `Thumbs.db`/`desktop.ini`) files are no longer captured into the store; any that got in previously are cleaned up on your next Capture.
- Reports and pickers now show real paths (no more `{configDir}/…`), and Pull/Push reports are titled with the remote's name.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.10.0...0.11.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
> ⚠️ **After upgrading, run Capture once** (on the device that owns your settings). This release renames a field inside `store.lock.json`; until the store is re-captured, **Apply fails with a validation error** ("store.lock.json must be {capturedAt: …}"). One Capture heals it everywhere. Pull/Push are unaffected.

## What's new

### Fixed
- **Browse button works now.** Clicking Browse… on a vault remote crashed with "failed module electron" — the bundled dynamic import couldn't be resolved by the Electron renderer. It now uses the standard `window.require` path; the folder dialog opens and the store path auto-fills.

### Improved
- **Version records survive a bad Capture.** If a plugin's config fails to capture (e.g. its file is missing), the store now keeps the last known plugin version instead of dropping it — so Apply's compatibility warnings on other devices stay meaningful.
- **Mobile settings are cleaner.** The Remotes tab is hidden on phones and tablets (remotes are a desktop feature), and the Core plugins tab uses the same icon as Obsidian's own settings.

### Internal
- The lock file field `publishedAt` is now `capturedAt`, matching the Capture vocabulary used everywhere else (this is the change behind the upgrade note above).

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.9.0...0.10.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### Remotes, redesigned (breaking)
- **Simpler model**: a remote is now *Another vault* (one **Store path** field — the store folder's absolute path) or a *Git repository* (URL, branch, optional folder in the repo). The old `local-path` + `root` split is gone.
- **Browse instead of typing**: pick the other vault in a system folder dialog and the store is located automatically (it finds `config-sync.json` for you).
- **Cleaner tab**: remotes render as compact rows that expand to edit, with a clearly visible "+ Add remote" row (same pattern for "+ Add rule" under Advanced).
- **One remote, zero friction**: with a single remote configured, Pull/Push run immediately — no picker dialog.

> **Breaking**: remotes configured in earlier versions are ignored (settings shape changed, no migration). Re-add them once under Settings → Remotes — Browse makes it a two-click job.

### Less noise
- The General tab's "Store transport" info row is gone; descriptions and command names now speak plainly (e.g. "Pull: get settings from a remote" instead of "Pull (remote → store)").
- README updated to match.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.8.0...0.9.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

### Settings panel
- **Discovered files keep their place**: turning on a discovered config file now keeps it in the Discovered section (name and path are fixed by the file); expand the row to adjust devices, sanitize patterns, or description. Turning it off returns it to the unsynced list. Custom rules is back to hand-written rules only.
- **Icon controls**: Reset all / Reset are now reset icons; Add rule / Add remote are + buttons on their section headings.
- **Phone-friendly layout**: tabs show icons (labels collapse on phones except the active tab), edit forms stack vertically on narrow screens, long paths truncate — no more horizontal panning on mobile.

### Directory review
- Added a "Security & privacy" section to the README disclosing the optional desktop-only remote features (git network use, out-of-vault local-path access).
- Releases now include auto-generated notes.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/xooooooooox/obsidian-config-sync/compare/0.7.0...0.8.0
